key_insight: Boron co-alloying with Sc in AlScN enables a more tunable AlScBN ferroelectric that lowers coercive field while preserving high remanent polarization and improving endurance.
engineering_relevance: The study points to a CMOS-compatible, scalable ferroelectric platform that can reduce dependence on scarce Sc while improving suitability for non-volatile memory devices.
method: Combinatorial gradient deposition by HiPIMS at 250°C
Automated screening of 850 unique AlScBN composition samples
XPS and combinatorial device library characterization
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a full compositional screening of 850 samples, with remanent polarization of 130–150 μC/cm2 and coercive field reduced from 7 to 3 MV/cm through Sc and B co-doping.
watch_item: Performance trends are derived from thin-film combinatorial libraries, so device-scale uniformity, long-term reliability, and process transfer to production flows still need validation.
paper_title: Boron Co-Alloying in AlScN Wurtzite Ferroelectrics: Insights from an 850-Sample Combinatorial Study
